title: Lean Human Infrastructure
definition: A minimal yet functional team structure for launching, sustaining, and growing a network effectively.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Creating a small, efficient team of essential roles to start and grow a project while keeping costs low.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A lean structure that focuses on the core roles necessary for a project's success and prioritizes scalability and adaptability.
difficulty_levels.expert: An innovative approach to building human infrastructure by minimizing overhead, emphasizing flexibility, and leveraging part-time roles and stipends to foster growth and impact.

extra_fields.insight: Balancing idealism with realism: outlining a lean human structure for a philosophical project to transition from vision to operational reality.
